article_id: Article 1. Nomenclature of the State
text_snippet: This Constitution establishes a Federal and Democratic State structure. Accordingly, the Ethiopian state shall be known as The Federal Democratic Republic of Ethiopia.

article_id: Article 10. Human and Democratic Rights
text_snippet: 1. Human rights and freedoms, emanating from the nature of mankind, are inviolable and inalienable. 2. Human and democratic rights of citizens and peoples shall be respected.

article_id: Article 100. Directives on Taxation

article_id: Article 106. The Version with Final Legal Authority
text_snippet: The Amharic version of this Constitution shall have final legal authority.

article_id: Article 11. Separation of State and Religion
text_snippet: 1. State and religion are separate. 2. There shall be no state religion. 3. The state shall not interfere in religious matters and religion shall not interfere in state affairs.

article_id: Article 13. Scope of Application and Interpretation

article_id: Article 14. Rights to life, the Security of Person and Liberty
text_snippet: Every person has the inviolable and inalienable right to life, the security of person and liberty.

article_id: Article 15. Right to Life
text_snippet: Every person has the right to life. No person may be deprived of his life except as a punishment for a serious criminal offence determined by law.

article_id: Article 16. The Right of the Security of Person
text_snippet: Everyone has the right to protection against bodily harm.

article_id: Article 17. Right to Liberty
text_snippet: 1. No one shall be deprived of his or her liberty except on such grounds and in accordance with such procedure as are established by law. 2. No person may be subjected to arbitrary arrest, and no pers

article_id: Article 18. Prohibition against Inhuman Treatment

text_snippet: 1. Persons arrested have the right to be informed promptly, in a language they understand, of the reasons for their arrest and of any charge against them. 2. Persons arrested have the right to remain

article_id: Article 2. Ethiopian Territorial Jurisdiction
text_snippet: The territorial jurisdiction of Ethiopia shall comprise the territory of the members of the Federation and its boundaries shall be as determined by international agreements.

article_id: Article 23. Prohibition of Double Jeopardy
text_snippet: No person shall be liable to be tried or punished again for an offence for which he has already been finally convicted or acquitted in accordance with the criminal law and procedure.

article_id: Article 26. Right to Privacy
text_snippet: 1. Everyone has the right to privacy. This right shall include the right not to be subjected to searches of his home, person or property, or the seizure of any property under his personal possession.

article_id: Article 8. Sovereignty of the People

article_id: Article 88. Political Objectives

article_id: Article 95. Revenue
text_snippet: The Federal Government and the States shall share revenue taking the federal arrangement into account.
